title: Piers Plowman (B version)
summary: The repository XML is a copy of a 1993 U-M/OTA electronic text derived from A. V. C. Schmidt's 1978 critical B-text. Schmidt explicitly based that edition on Trinity College Cambridge MS B.15.17 (W), while also supplying selected variant readings from other witnesses not yet identified passage by passage from a complete copy. A complete 156-canvas Trinity IIIF facsimile is publicly available and cached at a 1600-pixel-wide derivative profile; the poem occupies ff. 1r–130v, canvases 5–135, within the larger codex. The Piers Plowman Electronic Archive's documentary W edition is modeled as collateral scholarship, not as an upstream source of PPlLan.
review_notes: The B-version work, Trinity B.15.17, Schmidt's critical edition, the U-M/OTA encoding, and the General Corpus XML are distinct layers.
Schmidt's text is based on W but is not modeled as a diplomatic transcription because its title statement explicitly advertises selected variant readings.
The complete cached IIIF bundle covers the provider's published 156-canvas codex surrogate; the work portion ends on the left side of the shared f. 130v–f. 131r spread.
Current OTA catalog access, denied anonymous bitstreams, the dead replacement pointer, legacy personal-scholarly-use language, and the current OTA Academic Use terms are preserved separately.
The repository's root Unlicense speaks of software; its application to inherited XML data and markup is not broadened without file-specific evidence.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for PPlLan
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: The source submodule is a separate repository and is not modified by this manifest.

entities.label: U-M/OTA PPlLan electronic text
alternate_labels: The Vision of Piers Plowman machine-readable text
entities.description: A machine-readable Middle English B-text prepared from Schmidt's 1978 edition and historically distributed through the Oxford Text Archive and University of Michigan.
notes: The XML header records both U-M and OTA availability statements but does not identify the individual encoder or keying workflow.
The current OTA item points to replacement accession 3261, whose purl resolved to a 404 during this review.

entities.label: A. V. C. Schmidt's 1978 critical edition of the B text
alternate_labels: The Vision of Piers Plowman: a critical edition of the B-text based on Trinity College Cambridge MS B.15.17 with selected variant readings

bibliographic.citation: William Langland, The Vision of Piers Plowman: A Critical Edition of the B-Text Based on Trinity College Cambridge MS B.15.17 with Selected Variant Readings, ed. A. V. C. Schmidt (London: J. M. Dent; New York: E. P. Dutton, 1978).
bibliographic.title: The Vision of Piers Plowman
bibliographic.publisher: J. M. Dent; E. P. Dutton

notes: The XML header gives xlviii, 364 pages; public catalogs vary slightly over preliminary and total pagination, so the discrepancy is retained rather than silently normalized.
The edition is explicitly critical and W-based, with selected variants. A full copy was not anonymously inspectable, so additional witness sigla and supplied readings remain unresolved.

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge MS B.15.17
entities.description: A fourteenth-century parchment codex whose first booklet preserves the B version of Piers Plowman at ff. 1r–130v; subsequent leaves contain a Treatise of Sin and a short poem.

date_statements.label: production
date_statements.notes: The PPEA introduction refines the hand to around the turn of the fifteenth century; this manifest preserves the holding catalog's broader object date.

holding.notes: The official catalog places Piers Plowman at ff. 1–130v and the Treatise of Sin at ff. 131–147r; the exact IIIF labels normalize the poem's opening as f. 1r.

notes: The official catalog reports 28–30 lines per page and gives a detailed collation.
The work occupies almost the whole codex but not the later texts or binding surfaces.

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge complete published IIIF facsimile of MS B.15.17
entities.description: The official 156-canvas color digital surrogate, including front and back covers and the manuscript sequence exposed by Trinity's Presentation manifest.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and Image API JPEG derivatives
notes: Complete means every canvas in Trinity's published 156-canvas surrogate was captured; it does not claim preservation-master resolution or every possible physical angle of the codex.

entities.label: Piers Plowman Electronic Archive documentary edition of W
alternate_labels: Trinity College Cambridge MS B.15.17 digital documentary edition
entities.description: A scholarly documentary digital edition and manuscript study of W, consulted to verify the codex boundary and W's editorial role. It is collateral to, not an upstream source of, PPlLan.

entities.label: Internet Archive controlled-access scan of Schmidt's 1978 edition
entities.description: An Internet Archive scan record for the exact 1978 edition. Public metadata identifies the book, but PDF and OCR files were access-restricted and the scan contents were not inspected anonymously.
entities.reproduction_format: Controlled digital lending page images and encrypted package
notes: The public metadata and restricted-file responses establish the access route, not page-level completeness or the exact copy's internal condition.

agents.name: William Langland
agents.dates: fl. late fourteenth century; traditional attribution
notes: The conventional name is retained with attribution uncertainty; the manifest does not claim a securely identified biography.

scope.notes: The matching internal identifier, OTA accession, header, title, source statement, and text support the derivation; the exact import date into the CME git repository is undocumented.

subject.description: The electronic B-version text: Prologue and Passus I–XX

object.description: Schmidt's edited Middle English poem, excluding printed front matter, back matter, and editorial notes

scope.notes: The XML header explicitly names the 1978 edition and the omitted editorial matter.

object.description: The Piers Plowman B text in W

scope.notes: W is the declared base manuscript. The relation is not diplomatic or exclusive because the edition also advertises selected variant readings from other witnesses.

subject.description: All 156 canvases of Trinity's published codex surrogate

object.description: The bound manuscript, including adjacent texts and binding surfaces represented in the surrogate

subject.description: PPEA's documentary edition of the W text

scope.notes: This documentary edition was consulted as collateral evidence and is not placed in PPlLan's upstream derivation chain.

subject.description: Controlled-access scan package whose internal page sequence was not anonymously inspectable

object.description: The 1978 printed edition as identified by the provider metadata

scope.notes: The provider metadata identifies the exact edition, but access restrictions prevented a page-level completeness audit.

access.access_method: Read the checked-out CME submodule path
U-M persistent name resolver to the digital item
notes: The resolver remains the exact legacy route, but its current Quod destination blocked automated inspection with an HTTP 403 challenge. That check does not prove that human access is unavailable.

access.access_method: Current Oxford Text Archive item page
notes: The item page is public. Its replacement pointer to accession 3261 resolved to a current 404 and is retained as a failed legacy route rather than a verified successor.

access.access_method: Exact DSpace bitstream routes advertised by the item record
notes: Anonymous GET requests redirected to the item page instead of delivering bytes; no file was cached. The review did not bypass authentication or infer that a login would necessarily grant access.

access.access_method: WorldCat bibliographic record and holdings discovery
access.format: Catalog records for the print book
notes: These are bibliographic and holdings routes, not an open full-text copy of the exact edition.

access.access_method: Internet Archive controlled digital lending item
access.format: Restricted page images and encrypted lending files
notes: The metadata endpoint was public, but direct PDF and OCR requests returned HTTP 401. No restricted file was cached or treated as inspected.

access.access_method: Trinity College Cambridge Medieval Manuscripts catalogue
Official manuscript viewer and exact 156-canvas IIIF Presentation manifest

local_copies.label: Complete Trinity College Cambridge MS B.15.17 IIIF facsimile bundle

work_portion.label: Piers Plowman (B version)
locators: Trinity College Cambridge MS B.15.17, ff. 1r–130v
notes: Canvas 5 is the opening f. 1r text leaf.
Canvas 135 is a shared spread: Piers Plowman ends on f. 130v at left and the Treatise of Sin begins on f. 131r at right. The complete bundle deliberately retains both sides.
Complete coverage of every canvas in the provider's 156-canvas Presentation manifest, including front and back covers and adjacent texts outside the work portion.
All 156 archived image members match the inventory byte counts and SHA-256 values; unzip integrity testing passed.
Every image used the non-upscaling IIIF request profile full/1600,/0/default.jpg. These are practical access derivatives, not preservation masters.
Representative inspection confirmed full-color photography at the front cover, f. 1r, the f. 130v–f. 131r boundary, and the back cover.

access.format: IIIF Presentation 2 and Image API JPEG derivatives
notes: The ignored local cache is a private research copy and does not itself authorize redistribution beyond Trinity's item-level license.

access.access_method: Piers Plowman Electronic Archive W edition and manuscript introduction

rights.component: The underlying fourteenth-century Piers Plowman B-version text
rights.basis: The underlying work was composed in 1377–1379, centuries before modern copyright; the U.S. duration framework does not extend copyright to the medieval text itself.
notes: This statement covers neither Schmidt's modern editorial choices nor the U-M/OTA encoding, manuscript photography, or interface assets.

rights.component: Schmidt's 1978 edited readings, introduction, glosses, commentary, apparatus, and other original editorial contributions
rights.jurisdiction: United States and other applicable jurisdictions
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1978 and no public-domain dedication or open license for its original editorial contribution was verified.
notes: This does not place Langland's underlying medieval work under copyright.

rights.component: The U-M/OTA machine-readable text, transcription choices, and electronic markup
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: The legacy header limits OTA use to personal scholarly purposes, while the current OTA item carries an Academic Use label and the current OTA license permits noncommercial research and teaching subject to its conditions. No item-specific open license was verified.
notes: Public catalog access and academic-use permission are not equivalent to unrestricted commercial redistribution.
The current bitstreams were not anonymously retrievable, so their embedded terms could not be compared directly with the repository XML.

rights.component: The checked-in PPlLan XML artifact and inherited electronic markup
rights.jurisdiction: Repository terms, source-provider terms, and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: At the pinned CME revision, the root LICENSE is the Unlicense and explicitly speaks of software. No file-specific notice was found establishing that its public-domain dedication applies to inherited textual data or markup, while the XML preserves OTA personal-scholarly-use language and derives from a 1978 critical edition.
notes: The repository license is recorded rather than ignored, but its software wording is not broadened to third-party text, editorial contribution, or provider markup without an explicit scope statement.
The Unlicense does not transfer to Schmidt's edition, Langland's underlying work, or Trinity's photographs.

rights.component: Trinity College Cambridge manuscript photographs and IIIF image files
rights.jurisdiction: Worldwide, subject to the item-level license
rights.basis: The exact manuscript catalog and IIIF manifest identify the images as copyright the Master and Fellows of Trinity College Cambridge and license them under CC BY-NC 4.0.
notes: The license applies to Trinity's images, not to Schmidt's critical edition or the physical manuscript as an object.
Attribution and noncommercial conditions remain relevant to any redistribution of the cached derivatives.

editorial_practices.description: The electronic text retains the edited Middle English B-version poem at printed pp. 1–263 but omits front matter, back matter, and Schmidt's editorial notes.
notes: The encoding therefore cannot substitute for Schmidt's introduction or apparatus when evaluating base-witness and variant readings.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Variant readings in witnesses other than the declared base manuscript W
editorial_practices.representation: Selected variant readings in a critical B-text and apparatus
editorial_practices.description: Schmidt based the critical B-text on Trinity B.15.17 while supplying selected variant readings. The public title statements establish the practice but do not identify all contributing witness sigla or passage ranges.
editorial_practices.scope: The edited B text and its apparatus

evidence.summary: Identifies PPlLan, OTA U-1687-A, the 1993 electronic edition, Schmidt's 1978 source edition, the W-based critical B-text with selected variants, personal-scholarly-use terms, omitted front/back matter and editorial notes, and the Prologue plus twenty Passus at printed pp. 1–263.
notes: Git blob 293d11257ad431f4c652fb8ae6cbeb22d22957cd at CME submodule commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.

evidence.citation: Oxford Text Archive, item 1687, The Vision of Piers Plowman.
evidence.summary: Provides the current public metadata record, Langland heading, Middle English language, Academic Use label, file names, and replacement pointer. Anonymous requests to the advertised text and XML-header bitstreams redirected to the item page, and the replacement purl resolved to a 404.
notes: No bitstream bytes were retrieved or cached.

evidence.summary: States the conditions for academic access, duplication, noncommercial research and teaching, acknowledgment, authorized-user access, and permission for uses outside those terms.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan persistent name resolver for PPlLan.
evidence.summary: The exact U-M resolver identified in the XML redirected to the Quod interface, which returned an HTTP 403 automation challenge during this review; the result supports an unknown automated-access status, not a claim of human unavailability.

evidence.citation: WorldCat, The Vision of Piers Plowman, OCLC 5221733.
evidence.summary: Confirms Schmidt's editorial responsibility, the 1978 Dent/Dutton publication, the exact critical B-text subtitle, the Trinity B.15.17 base manuscript, selected variant readings, and bibliographic identifiers. The records differ slightly over preliminary pagination.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive, visionofpiersplo0000lang_y7q3, The Vision of Piers Plowman (1978).
evidence.summary: The public item metadata identifies the exact 1978 edition and an access-restricted lending item. Public metadata and scan-data files were visible, but direct PDF and OCR requests returned HTTP 401; the scan itself was not inspected or cached.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge, Medieval Manuscripts Catalogue, MS B.15.17 (James 353).
evidence.summary: Describes the fourteenth-century parchment codex, 147-folio extent, dimensions, collation, language, contents, Piers Plowman at ff. 1–130v, subsequent texts at ff. 131–147, and item-level image copyright and CC BY-NC 4.0 license.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge, MS B.15.17, direct IIIF Presentation manifest.
evidence.summary: Provides 156 labeled canvases from front cover through back cover, exact Image API services, Trinity attribution, and the item-level CC BY-NC 4.0 license. Canvas labels map the poem to canvases 5–135.

evidence.citation: Direct inventory, checksum, ZIP-integrity, and representative image inspection of the cached Trinity College Cambridge MS B.15.17 IIIF bundle.
evidence.locator: manifest.json; inventory.json; images/000001.jpg; images/000005.jpg; images/000135.jpg; images/000156.jpg
evidence.summary: Verified 158 ZIP members including 156 unique inventory-backed images, every archived image's byte count and SHA-256, a uniform 1600-pixel request profile, and the work boundaries. Canvas 5 is f. 1r; canvas 135 is the shared f. 130v–f. 131r spread where the poem ends and the next codex item begins. Cover and boundary samples are full-color photographs.

evidence.citation: Piers Plowman Electronic Archive, About Piers Plowman.
evidence.summary: Describes the alliterative allegorical dream vision attributed to William Langland and its A, B, and C versions.

evidence.citation: Piers Plowman Electronic Archive, Trinity College Cambridge MS B.15.17 (W), Introduction.
evidence.summary: Identifies W, describes Piers Plowman in Booklet 1 at ff. 1–130 and other texts in Booklet 2 at ff. 131–147, and records W's central role as base manuscript for Schmidt and earlier critical editions.

evidence.citation: Piers Plowman Electronic Archive, W Preface.
evidence.summary: Explains W's importance among B witnesses and its central role in modern B-text editorial practice, while presenting PPEA's own documentary edition as a distinct digital object.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME, root LICENSE at commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: The pinned root license contains the Unlicense and explicitly describes a public-domain dedication for software. No path-specific statement was found extending that wording to inherited textual data or XML markup.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright.
evidence.summary: Provides the U.S. duration framework used to distinguish the fourteenth-century underlying work from possible copyright in Schmidt's 1978 original editorial contribution.

open_questions.question: Which witnesses supplied Schmidt's selected variant readings, how were they used, and how should the edition's intended lineation be reconciled with the XML's duplicate and missing N labels?
next_steps: Borrow or inspect a complete copy of Schmidt's exact 1978 edition, including the full introduction, manuscript list, apparatus, and lineation statement.
Record contributing witness entities and collated_against relations only when their sigla and passage scopes can be verified.
Compare the p. 188 running-title artifact, duplicate-key Latin continuations, and missing numbered positions against the printed pages before asserting a canonical verse-line total.
notes: The exact Internet Archive scans located were controlled-access items; no authentication or access control was bypassed.

open_questions.question: Who keyed and encoded PPlLan, from which physical copy of Schmidt 1978, and what corrections or transformations occurred between the 1993 OTA/U-M text and the CME git artifact?
next_steps: Ask OTA and U-M for accession, production, transfer, and revision records for U-1687-A / PPlLan.
Ask Kyvero Press whether the CME import preserved the provider bytes or applied transformations before the recorded git blob.
Keep copied_from supported rather than assigning undocumented encoder agents or production dates.

open_questions.question: What current item-level permission governs reuse of the PPlLan transcription and markup, and is OTA accession 3261 a recoverable replacement for item 1687?
next_steps: Ask OTA why the advertised item-1687 bitstreams deny anonymous delivery and whether accession 3261 was withdrawn, renumbered, or can be restored.
Ask OTA and U-M for the original permission record covering Schmidt's 1978 edited text and for an item-specific statement on commercial redistribution.
Ask Kyvero Press whether the root Unlicense was intended to cover CME XML data and markup as well as software, without treating that answer as authority over third-party editorial rights.
